evidence_excerpt: The patient showed a complex phenotype progressing throughout the years from Noonan syndrome at birth towards LEOPARD syndrome and Noonan-like/multiple giant cell lesion syndrome, with persistent issues including severe hearing loss, skeletal hypomineralisation, and incomplete pubertal development at long-term follow-up.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Ongoing significant issues include bilateral severe sensorineural hearing loss, generalized hypomineralisation with cyst-like mandibular lesions, and incomplete development of secondary sexual characteristics.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: At 7 months of age, bilateral severe sensorineural hearing loss and delayed psychomotor development mainly in the motor area was noticed.
At the age of 4 years, she underwent cardiac catheterisation and pulmonary valve angioplasty.
At the age of 4 years, failure to thrive was reported.
At 4 years she developed multiple lentigines and multiple café-au-lait spots; a diagnosis of LEOPARD syndrome was proposed.
Only with increasing age, the development of multiple lentigines, sensorineural deafness and hypertrophic cardiomyopathy prompted the diagnosis of full-blown LEOPARD syndrome.
At 10 years, pulmonary valve stenosis surgical correction with patch was performed.
A skeletal survey showed generalised hypomineralisation with thickened trabeculae and cyst-like lesions in the mandible, leading to a diagnosis of Noonan-like/multiple giant cell lesion syndrome.
At present, she presents incomplete development of secondary sexual characteristics with small breasts and hypoplastic external genitalia.

notes_on_uncertainty: A precise index date of definitive diagnosis is not documented; the first specialist evaluation at 1 month was used as the proxy. Timing categories are approximated relative to this reference. Improvement after cardiac interventions or resolution of early motor delay is not explicitly documented; functional status and symptom burden are inferred conservatively from long-term descriptions.
